title: AI in the SDLC: Rethinking AI Coding Tools & AI Agents
summary: Cedric Clyburn argues that while AI promises speed in software development, productivity gains are limited despite faster coding. He proposes redesigning SDLC workflows with AI agents to improve testing, outcomes, and delivery.
writeup: Cedric Clyburn examines where AI fits in the software development lifecycle, arguing that faster code generation has not translated into proportional productivity gains. The core claim: writing code quicker is only one part of delivery, so the broader workflow needs rethinking. He proposes redesigning SDLC workflows around AI agents to strengthen testing, outcomes, and delivery rather than focusing on coding speed alone.
